[2018-03-20 16:17] .jrkirby (jrkirby): I don't think it would cause stalemates, because there's only 1 max supply. It's like adding 4 HP to the max absorber in the set for 7 drones, a blastforge and a half animus. That's 29 gold to counter 4 attack per turn. And wall of fortune shows that even increasing absorb indefinitely (at an increasing price) won't cause stalemates. It just causes more drones to be optimal. This would be similar - probably add around 10 to 15 drones to the optimal drone count.

For this to cause a real stalemate, the max attackers either player could get would have to be (set max absorb) + 4. Max absorb usually caps at around 6 or 7. In the worst case each player can get 10 tarsiers, 10 steelsplitters, and 10 gauss cannon, so you'd have to have around 6 supply of this unit or similar units for a high econ stalemate to be a thing in any set - and most sets have many more permanent attackers.

TL;DR: would just make long games, not cause stalemates.
[2018-03-20 16:21] darkeisbein: it seems like a weird unit, like it just does not a lot as long as better absorbers are in the set
[2018-03-20 16:21] darkeisbein: and the special condition only becomes relevant if soak supply is an issue in the set
[2018-03-20 16:23] supergreyman (Greyman): I'm not sure what interesting effect it will have if you compare this one with regenerative property, to the same one but with supply of 10.
[2018-03-20 16:24] supergreyman (Greyman): It handicaps you from making multiple of the same one, but we got Doomed Wall for the same prompt 4HP defense.
[2018-03-20 16:24] .jrkirby (jrkirby): It's a newbie trap :LongNoseLiar:
[2018-03-20 16:24] darkeisbein: it changes the way you play, like you can try to get it in early to extent the duration you can play multiple walls a turn
[2018-03-20 16:24] darkeisbein: except that never matters
[2018-03-20 16:25] supergreyman (Greyman): Expert Player Trap too :LongNoseLiar:
[2018-03-20 16:26] .jrkirby (jrkirby): There are definitely sets where infinite supply of absorbers is good. These are usually sets which have >4 absorb. In the other sets, this is a 4 HP absorb. So I think it's relevant in most sets.
[2018-03-20 16:26] supergreyman (Greyman): That should be our next challenge idea: Create a Unit that is both newbie trap and expert player trap.
[2018-03-20 16:27] .bky_1556 (bky): Isn't practically every unit a newbie trap?
[2018-03-20 16:27] .jrkirby (jrkirby): Also, it's different than a 10 supply of the same unit, because you can only get 1 at a time.
[2018-03-20 16:27] darkeisbein: I mean you can never have enough tantalum rays
[2018-03-20 16:28] .bky_1556 (bky): Tantalum rays' newbie trap has to do with click policy.
[2018-03-20 16:28] supergreyman (Greyman): I think it needs an additional effect to make it more unique.
[2018-03-20 16:29] .jrkirby (jrkirby): IDK, I think "Theoretically infinite soak" is unique enough
[2018-03-20 16:30] supergreyman (Greyman): Because if you use it for soak, Doomed Wall does it better with its lower cost.
[2018-03-20 16:30] .jrkirby (jrkirby): doomed wall can be exploited
[2018-03-20 16:30] darkeisbein: you can exploit this one too
[2018-03-20 16:30] .jrkirby (jrkirby): and doomed wall can run out of supply
[2018-03-20 16:30] darkeisbein: now it's out of supply
[2018-03-20 16:31] .jrkirby (jrkirby): only the turn it's frozen
[2018-03-20 16:31] darkeisbein: it is a unique, new way of exploiting though
[2018-03-20 16:31] supergreyman (Greyman): I just don't like the idea of having the game more prolonged, because that's the very reason why supply exists.
[2018-03-20 16:31] supergreyman (Greyman): It used to have infinite supply.
[2018-03-20 16:31] .jrkirby (jrkirby): So you don't like Energy Matrix or Defense grid?
[2018-03-20 16:32] supergreyman (Greyman): No, I'm saying about the supply.
[2018-03-20 16:32] supergreyman (Greyman): Although I dislike them a little too.
[2018-03-20 16:33] .jrkirby (jrkirby): I think the "Only 1 a turn" is enough of a stipulation to keep game lengths in check
[2018-03-20 16:33] .jrkirby (jrkirby): If many similar units were printed it would be a problem
[2018-03-20 16:33] .jrkirby (jrkirby): but that has an easy solution - don't print more units like this
[2018-03-20 16:37] supergreyman (Greyman): It's like.....there are situations where economy is exhausted on both players. One player has Doomed Wall with another blocker that's more permanent, but doesn't have a lot of attackers. The other player has a lot of attackers, and the only way to get through the defense is by making them run out of supply.
[2018-03-20 16:38] .bky_1556 (bky): In other words it results in draws that can evade draw detection.
[2018-03-20 16:38] masn6811 (Masne98888): man, that is such a subtle shalev's rule break
[2018-03-20 16:39] .jrkirby (jrkirby): @Masne98888 I'm glad you appreciated the point of the unit 😄
[2018-03-20 16:40] masn6811 (Masne98888): though i think it should probably be axed, because infinite supply leads to stalemates
[2018-03-20 16:41] masn6811 (Masne98888): did you know that in base+electrovore, perfect play from both sides results in a draw if supply was infinite?
[2018-03-20 16:41] .jrkirby (jrkirby): I did not know that. Makes some sense though
[2018-03-20 16:42] darkeisbein: I don't know if it would lead to a significant amount of draws
[2018-03-20 16:43] .jrkirby (jrkirby): I really doubt this unit would cause a draw though. Cause all any player would need to do is get to 10+ attack and the game will definitely end
[2018-03-20 16:43] darkeisbein: like if both sides not being able to kill 4 health is not much more likely then both sides being unable to kill  blastforge
[2018-03-20 16:44] darkeisbein: ebb turbine is similarly draw inducing

[2018-03-20 16:44] darkeisbein: yeah but in what situation do you get breached and then have enough money left over to build this + an absorber but attack is stalled?
[2018-03-20 16:45] darkeisbein: I mean you still need 7 econ to buy it every turn
[2018-03-20 16:45] darkeisbein: otherwise it's just a 4 health absorber
[2018-03-20 16:45] .bky_1556 (bky): Power it with Ebb Turbine?
[2018-03-20 16:45] darkeisbein: so basically both sides need to get breached, have 7 econ left over and no attacker supply left
[2018-03-20 16:46] .bky_1556 (bky): This + Chrono Filter + Xaetron vs. 6 attack is fun.

--- Thread (50 messages, 2018-03-20) ---
[2018-03-20 16:46] darkeisbein: hm I guess that is an awkward situation if building attackers is the loosing move so both sides just keep buying this
[2018-03-20 16:47] .bky_1556 (bky): You need to alternate between soaking with it and blocking on just Xaetron
[2018-03-20 16:47] .bky_1556 (bky): while either gaining or losing 1 gold per 2 turns
[2018-03-20 16:48] .jrkirby (jrkirby): This situation is so contrived it would probably happen in 1 in a million games
[2018-03-20 16:48] .jrkirby (jrkirby): iceblades cause more draws
[2018-03-20 16:48] masn6811 (Masne98888): i forgot how it broke shalev's rule
[2018-03-20 16:48] masn6811 (Masne98888): what is the situation where you'd rather have it in supply than on the battlefield?
[2018-03-20 16:48] .jrkirby (jrkirby): When it dies, it's better for you
[2018-03-20 16:49] .jrkirby (jrkirby): technically breaks shalevs rule
[2018-03-20 16:49] .jrkirby (jrkirby): or does it not? That's why it's so subtle.
[2018-03-20 16:49] .bky_1556 (bky): If you'd rather have something in supply than on the battlefield, that's NOT a SDR break.
[2018-03-20 16:50] .bky_1556 (bky): Otherwise something like Centrifuge couldn't exist.
[2018-03-20 16:50] masn6811 (Masne98888): in this case, if that's the case, it causes an enemy gauss charge to break shalev's rule
[2018-03-20 16:50] masn6811 (Masne98888): so that's the difference between this unit and centifuge
[2018-03-20 16:50] masn6811 (Masne98888): an enemy gauss charge lets you transfer it back into supply, but not a centifuge of yours
[2018-03-20 16:51] masn6811 (Masne98888): so the only question is in what situation you want to transfer it into supply
[2018-03-20 16:52] .bky_1556 (bky): No SDR break, I think.
[2018-03-20 16:52] .jrkirby (jrkirby): Honestly IDK if it *really* breaks SDR. But it's on the line between not breaking SDR, and actually breaking it.
[2018-03-20 16:52] .bky_1556 (bky): Transferring it into supply only benefits you if you buy it later.
[2018-03-20 16:52] .jrkirby (jrkirby): It toys with the idea of breaking SDR
[2018-03-20 16:52] namington: I don't see a situation where you'd rather have it in supply than on board
[2018-03-20 16:53] masn6811 (Masne98888): but what's the case where you want to move it to the battlefield for 7BR?
[2018-03-20 16:53] .bky_1556 (bky): If you buy it later, SDR[units] implies that it's at least as good to have held it on  board the whole time.
[2018-03-20 16:53] masn6811 (Masne98888): like if it died in the meantime, you can just rebuy it then
[2018-03-20 16:53] .bky_1556 (bky): and then on the turn you buy it or later, SDR[gold] applies.
[2018-03-20 16:53] namington: I don't see how it breaks SDR
[2018-03-20 16:53] namington: It still seems always better to have it alive than dead
[2018-03-20 16:53] masn6811 (Masne98888): so i can see cases where it's the same to have it on the battlefield as supply, but not where it's better to have it in supply
[2018-03-20 16:54] .bky_1556 (bky): So, provided nothing else violates SDR[Units | Gold] this does not violate SDR.
[2018-03-20 16:54] namington: Yeah, the same or better I should say
[2018-03-20 16:55] supergreyman (Greyman): If you want your opponent's damage to be blocked by Omega Splitter, but your opponent has 2 Frostbites and 4 attacks.
[2018-03-20 16:55] masn6811 (Masne98888): but then you just lose it for free
[2018-03-20 16:55] masn6811 (Masne98888): it ends up the same as if you lost it earlier
[2018-03-20 16:55] .bky_1556 (bky): I suppose it's SDR [Units | Gold | Tech] because of things like that custom unit that died if you wasted R.
[2018-03-20 16:56] .bky_1556 (bky): Either way, I think I've proven this death effect can't violate SDR.
[2018-03-20 16:57] .jrkirby (jrkirby): I stick so tightly to the rules that even when I try to design something that breaks the rules, it still obeys them :NotLikeThis:
[2018-03-20 16:58] supergreyman (Greyman): There's nothing that really interacts with supply, so it's hard to think of one, if there is any.
[2018-03-20 16:59] supergreyman (Greyman): You always want it out instead of the supply.
[2018-03-20 16:59] .jrkirby (jrkirby): But then it breaks the other SDR: it's better to have more supply than to have less :LongNoseLiar:
[2018-03-20 17:00] namington: It still breaks the unnamed design principle of "avoid endless games"
[2018-03-20 17:00] namington: Although probably not to a severe extent
[2018-03-20 17:00] masn6811 (Masne98888): Hmm... I have this unit idea. Tell me if it's ridiculously broken.
[2018-03-20 17:00] darkeisbein: well endless games already happen
[2018-03-20 17:01] namington: Yeah but this unit would enable far more than any before it
[2018-03-20 17:01] namington: Still not a huge deal though
[2018-03-20 17:01] .jrkirby (jrkirby): I'm pretty sure this would cause fewer draws than iceblade, the current gold standard
[2018-03-20 17:01] darkeisbein: I still don't see how it would do that
[2018-03-20 17:01] namington: Nah, I'm pretty sure thisd draw a lot more games than iceblade
[2018-03-20 17:02] namington: Maybe I'm hesitant with the infinite supply thing
[2018-03-20 17:02] darkeisbein: what would be a really weird porblem would be if both sides can buy just exactly this every turn and doing it is the optimal play
